items.headline: Lithium mining stocks broadly fall, U.S. Albemarle down over 4%
items.snippet: On Thursday, lithium mining stocks broadly fell, U.S. Albemarle (ALB.US) down over 4%, Chilean chemical and mining (SQM.US) down 3%, Sigma Lithium (SGML.US) down over 2%, Lithium Argentina (LAR.US) down nearly 1%.

items.headline: Albemarle (ALB) Stock Could Be 24.6% Undervalued After Recent Share Price Weakness

items.headline: How Is Albemarle Stock Performance Compared to Other Material Stocks?

items.headline: Albemarle, the biggest beneficiary of expanding lithium demand... Investment opinion Buy - Citi
items.snippet: Citi has raised its investment opinion on Albemarle (ALB), a global lithium producer, from High Risk to Buy. The target price remains at 25. Citi adjusted its estimates to reflect the lithium market outlook and price assumptions ahead of the 2026 fiscal second-quarter earnings an

items.headline: Albemarle raised to buy at Citi with underappreciated growth opportunities ahead
items.snippet: Citi analyst Pat Cunningham is skeptical that the market will flip to a meaningful glut given current demand tailwinds. Even in a balanced market, Albemarle is one of the primary beneficiaries of structural lithium demand tailwinds given its high-quality assets, conversion networ

items.headline: Lithium Mining Market Generates $5.31 Billion in 2025 with Strong Long-Term Growth Outlook
items.snippet: The explosive global expansion of the electric vehicle industry is the single most powerful demand driver for lithium mining. Each electric vehicle requires significant quantities of battery-grade lithium carbonate or lithium hydroxide, creating direct and sustained demand growth
